title: campomanesia rufa
intro: Campomanesia rufa, also known as the red guavaberry or red cambuquira, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae, native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as skin infections, wounds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Campomanesia rufa include reducing inflammation, fighting against bacteria and fungi, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
